title: veronica peregrina subsp. typica
intro: Veronica peregrina subsp. typica, also known as the "Pilgrim's Speedwell" or "Common Speedwell", is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae, native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Veronica peregrina subsp. typica include its ability to soothe sore throats, calm digestive issues, and reduce fever, making it a popular herbal remedy. The plant can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
